name: Interactive ROI calculator
description: 'See how much time you could save' — personalised estimate
conversion_rate_data: Interactive calculators are among the highest-converting CTA formats in B2B marketing. According to Outgrow's analysis of 50,000+ forms, interactive content achieves an average conversion rate of 47.3% compared to 2.8% for traditional static forms — a 16.9x improvement. More conservatively, the Content Marketing Institute reports interactive content generates 2x the conversions of static content. HubSpot's 2025 benchmarks place interactive calculator conversion rates at 8.3% on average (versus 3.8% for gated PDFs). Demand Metric research confirms that 81% of B2B buyers prefer interactive content over static, and about 45% of marketers find interactive tools (quizzes, calculators, assessments) effective for lead generation. Interactive demos and calculators drive 38–52% higher demo-to-opportunity conversion compared to static formats (SaaS Hero, 2026). Lead conversion rates for calculators typically range from 20–60% depending on gating strategy — ungated calculators with optional email capture at the results stage tend to maximise engagement while still generating leads.
best_for_audience: Best suited for CEO/owners and operations directors of care home groups who are evaluating the business case for AI — they need to justify spend to boards or partners. Also effective for registered managers who want to quantify the time savings they could gain back for care delivery. The personalised output ('you could save X hours per week') creates an immediate emotional and rational connection. Less effective for frontline care staff who are not decision-makers on technology purchases. Particularly strong for multi-site operators who can input their number of homes, staff count, and current manual hours to see aggregated savings across their group.
friction_level: Low — the calculator itself is ungated, requiring no email or personal details to use. Users input basic operational data (number of residents, staff count, hours spent on admin tasks) and receive an instant personalised estimate. Email capture occurs optionally at the results stage ('Email me a detailed breakdown') which feels like a value exchange rather than a gate. This progressive disclosure approach minimises upfront friction while still generating leads. The commitment is a few clicks and 60–90 seconds of time.
industry_examples: ROI calculators are widely used in B2B SaaS and professional services. Salesforce offers an Agentforce ROI Calculator that estimates AI agent savings based on industry and team size. Writer.com provides an enterprise AI ROI calculator. Swimlane offers a security automation ROI calculator. In care-adjacent sectors: GetTalkative provides an Advanced ROI Calculator for contact centres showing personalised time and cost savings. AI4SP offers a multi-sector AI ROI calculator covering 18 industries. In care tech specifically, no major UK care software provider currently offers a public-facing ROI calculator — this represents a significant differentiation opportunity for Clarica. The closest examples are generic time-savings claims on competitor websites (e.g., Birdie claims 45% reduction in finance processing time, Log My Care claims care plans in minutes not hours) but none offer a personalised, interactive calculator experience.
care_sector_relevance: High relevance. Care home managers and owners are under intense financial pressure — the sector operates on thin margins with local authority fee rates that often don't cover costs. An ROI calculator that translates AI adoption into pounds saved and hours recovered speaks directly to their daily reality. The personalised output addresses the sector's scepticism about technology claims ('every software company says they'll save us time'). By letting the user input their own numbers — number of residents, staff hours on paperwork, current compliance costs — the estimate feels credible and specific rather than generic. Care managers are time-poor and non-technical, so the calculator must be simple (3–5 inputs maximum), visually clear, and avoid jargon. The DHSC's 2025 technology survey found 73% of providers cite set-up costs as a barrier to tech adoption, making an ROI calculator that demonstrates payback period particularly powerful.
ai_trust_factor: High. An ROI calculator directly addresses the key objection from care sector buyers: 'We can't afford AI' or 'We don't know if it's worth it.' By providing a personalised, transparent estimate of time and cost savings, the calculator builds trust through specificity rather than vague promises. It positions Clarica as confident enough in its offering to let buyers evaluate the numbers themselves — a signal of credibility. The calculator can also include a comparison element ('currently you spend X hours on care plans manually; with AI assistance this drops to Y') that demystifies what AI actually does in practical terms. This is particularly important in a sector where the GOV.UK December 2025 announcement showed digital records save 20 minutes per care worker per shift — the calculator can build on these government-validated figures to establish trust.
funnel_stage: Consideration — this CTA targets buyers who have identified a need but are evaluating whether AI is the right solution and whether the investment makes sense. It bridges the gap between awareness ('we have too much admin') and decision ('let's talk to Clarica'). The calculator output naturally leads to a next step CTA: 'Want to discuss how to achieve these savings? Book a call.'
mobile_optimization_priority: High priority. Care managers browse during breaks on phones, so the calculator must work flawlessly on mobile. Best practice: use large input fields with number pads, slider controls instead of text entry where possible, and a single-scroll design rather than multiple pages. The results should be visually compelling — a simple graphic showing hours saved per week, cost savings per year, and payback period. Ensure the 'Email me my results' CTA is thumb-friendly. Avoid complex charts or tables that don't render well on small screens. A vertical card layout with one input per screen works better on mobile than a desktop-style form.
